preview_text:
The model favors Australia at 44%, viewing them as the likeliest winner, while Paraguay's win probability registers at 26%. This 18-percentage-point gap reflects a meaningful but not dominant edge; the underdog remains a live threat in the match-up.

The draw emerges as plausible in the model's forecast at 31%, sitting between the two win probabilities and suggesting genuine competitive balance across the three outcomes. Australia's advantage is real but not commanding, leaving room for either team to secure points or resolve the contest in regulation.

A sharp divergence exists between the model and the sportsbook consensus. Books favor Paraguay to 44% and rate Australia at 27%, a roughly 17-percentage-point swing in the opposite direction from the model's view. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with sportsbooks on Paraguay and Australia, reinforcing that gap. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct; the disconnect warrants attention from users assessing their own views against both signals.
